q: Surface tension is the tendency of the surface of a liquid to behave like a stretched elastic skin.
Cohesion refers to forces between molecules of different substances.
Cohesion makes water molecules pull together making the water surface stretch like an elastic skin.
Surface tension is the reason a water strider is unable to stride along water surface and sinks immediately.
Surface tension is defined as the force per unit length that acts across any line on a surface tending to pull the surface apart.
The SI unit of surface tension is Pascal second ($Pa \cdot s$).
Capillary action is defined as the spontaneous flow of a liquid into a narrow tube or porous material.
Capillary action is caused solely by cohesive forces of the liquid and ignores adhesive forces between the liquid and tube material.
Adhesion refers to forces between molecules of different substances.
In order for wicking to occur, a tube needs to be sufficiently large in diameter.
The rise of water underneath the ground is an example of capillary action.
Liquids that wet the tube are depressed within thin tubes below the surface of the surrounding liquid.
Water is a liquid that wets glass capillary tubes while mercury is one that does not.
Capillarity occurs for all liquids regardless of whether they are wetting or non-wetting liquids.
The smaller the bore of a glass tube, the higher the water will rise.
The rise of water in a capillary tube occurs until there is a sufficient mass of liquid for intermolecular forces to overcome gravitational forces.
